rulings.Case Title: MARTIN RYAN VS. 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant 1001 California Street Condominium Homeowners Associations Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication, Of Plaintiffs Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 26, 2020 line 1. DEFENDANT 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant 1001 California Street Condominium Homeowners Associations Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication, Of Plaintiffs Third Amended Complaint Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication. Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Motion is treated as a motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the causes of actions for breach of governing documents (5th) and breach of Davis-Sterling Act (6th ) and is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege each element of this cause of action as to the failure to provide alternative heating to the unit upon termination of central heating. Motion for Summary Adjudication of the cause of action for Breach of Fiduciary Duty is GRANTED. There are no facts in dispute concerning this cause of action and Plaintiff cites no authority in the opposition suggesting that defendants are not entitled to a judgement as a matter of law based on the arguments and authorities cited in the moving papers. Motion for Summary Adjudication of the remaining causes of action is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist as to the representations made regarding the roof and regarding business judgment rule. Plaintiff to prepare an order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DORAN CONSTRUCTION & DESIGN INC. VS. ROBERT POYNTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action, And For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 26, 2020 line . DEFENDANT ROBERT POYNTER, NILI POYNTER Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action, And For Attorneys Fees Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion to Expunge Lis Pendens is GRANTED. Plaintiff. Doran Construction & Design, Inc., failed to demonstrate probability of prevailing on the merits of its partition claim. The complaint does not allege and evidence does not show that Construction & Design Inc, was a co-owner of the property, entitled to partition. Request for attorneys' fees is granted. = (501/CFH)

If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. Defendant's Motion to Strike Punitive Damages. is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts , not conclusions, giving rise to the request for punitive damages. Such facts shall be alleged with requisite particularity showing that the moving defendant acted with a conscious disregard of plaintiff's rights. = (501/CFH)
